extraction.one_line_summary: BAUHAUS Phil won bunch sprint after early crash, MATTHEWS Michael captured 5 bonus seconds at intermediate sprints to close GC gap from 14s to 6s behind BETTIOL.
tactical_patterns: MATTHEWS Michael prioritized intermediate sprint bonifications over stage win, tactically targeting GC via time bonuses on flat stage.
PETERS Nans solo breakaway absorbed only after gaining KOM points, suggesting early-stage points jerseys undefended in opener circuits.
UAE train crashed approaching intermediate sprint while controlling, revealing over-commitment to minor time bonuses creates positional risk.
PLAPP Luke attacked for KOM points despite Australian road champion jersey, indicating domestique role prioritizes classification over stage result.

rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: recovered from early heavy crash to win sprint, showing resilience but vulnerability to crashes on circuit finishes
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: executed dual intermediate sprint strategy netting 5s GC gain, tactical discipline prioritizing bonifications over stage hunting
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: neutral: defended leader jersey but lost 6s to bonification hunting, UAE train crashed chasing intermediate points
rider_signals.PETERS Nans: neutral: lone early attacker for KOM points suggests climber testing form in season opener without GC threat
rider_signals.STRONG Corbin: positive: contested both intermediate sprints against faster finishers, aggressive points hunting from breakaway specialist
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: crashed early requiring bandaging, compromised sprint finish indicates injury impact on performance
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: second place sprint after using MATTHEWS as leadout reference from Schwalbe Classic, learning curve visible
